title: Lush Dense American Yautia Skillet
description: A pungently flavored, dense skillet dish featuring yautia, smoky bacon, and bold herbs, perfect for a lazy Sunday dinner. The iron teppan delivers a crisp crust while the interior stays tender.
instructions: Preheat iron teppan over medium-high heat until hot.
In a bowl, toss diced yautia with smoked paprika, salt, pepper, and 1 tbsp butter; set aside.
Cook diced bacon in teppan until crisp, then remove and set aside, leaving bacon fat in pan.
Add remaining butter to teppan and sauté onions until translucent, about 3 minutes.
Stir in minced garlic and cook until fragrant, about 30 seconds.
Add yautia to pan, spread evenly, and sear for 5 minutes on each side until golden brown.
Sprinkle fresh thyme and lemon zest over yautia, then add cooked bacon back to pan.
Cook for an additional 2 minutes, allowing flavors to meld, then serve hot.
